title: Missing Semicolon After Class
description: Find the line with the syntax error.
explanation: A class definition must end with a semicolon after the closing brace: '};'.

title: cout Without Namespace
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: cout and endl are in the std namespace. Use 'std::cout' and 'std::endl' or add 'using namespace std;'.

title: Wrong Include for String
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: std::string requires '#include <string>'. While some compilers include it indirectly via <iostream>, this is not guaranteed.

title: Vector Out of Range
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Index 3 is out of bounds for a vector of size 3. Valid indices are 0, 1, 2. Use v.at(3) for bounds-checked access.

title: Missing Virtual Destructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Deleting a derived object through a base pointer without a virtual destructor is undefined behavior. ~Base should be declared virtual.

title: Slicing Problem
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Assigning a Cat to an Animal variable causes object slicing. The Cat-specific part is lost, and a.speak() calls Animal::speak(), printing '...'.

title: Dangling Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Returning a reference to a local variable. The string 'name' is destroyed when the function returns, creating a dangling reference.

title: Missing Copy Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Default copy constructor does a shallow copy of the pointer 'data'. Both a and b point to the same memory, causing double-free on destruction.

title: Most Vexing Parse
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 'Timer t()' is parsed as a function declaration, not object creation. This is the 'most vexing parse'. Use 'Timer t;' or 'Timer t{};'.

title: Iterator Invalidation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Erasing invalidates the iterator. After erase, 'it' is invalid and ++it is undefined behavior. Use 'it = v.erase(it)' and skip ++it.

title: Private Inheritance Access
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: With private inheritance, Base's public members become private in Derived. Calling d.hello() from outside the class is not allowed.

title: Uninitialized Member in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Member 'height' is not initialized in the constructor's initializer list. Its value is indeterminate, making area() return garbage.

title: Const Method Missing
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: getCount() is not declared const, but it's called on a const reference. Non-const methods can't be called on const objects. Add 'const' after getCount().

title: Static Member Undefined
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Static member 'maxRetries' is declared but not defined. Add 'int Config::maxRetries;' outside the class to provide the definition.

title: Template Syntax Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Template argument deduction fails because 1 is int and 2.5 is double — T can't be both. Use add<double>(1, 2.5) or change to two template parameters.

title: Implicit Conversion in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 5.0 is implicitly converted to Meter via the single-argument constructor. While this works, it's often unintended. Mark the constructor 'explicit' to prevent it.

title: Using Moved-From Object
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: After std::move, 'a' is in a valid but unspecified state. While a.size() is safe (returns 0), accessing a[0] on an empty vector is undefined behavior.

title: Diamond Inheritance Ambiguity
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Ambiguous access: 'value' exists in both A::Base and B::Base. Use virtual inheritance or qualify: obj.A::value.

title: Lambda Capture by Reference Dangling
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The lambda captures 'count' by reference, but 'count' is a local variable destroyed when makeCounter returns. The lambda holds a dangling reference.

title: Missing Return in Operator Overload
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing 'return' keyword. The line creates a temporary Vec2 but doesn't return it. Should be 'return Vec2(x + other.x, y + other.y);'.

title: Narrowing Conversion in Braces
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Brace initialization does not allow narrowing conversions. Converting double to int loses data and is ill-formed in C++11.

title: Wrong Smart Pointer Usage
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Two separate shared_ptrs managing the same raw pointer will both try to delete it, causing double free. Use make_shared or copy the first shared_ptr.

title: Abstract Class Instantiation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Shape is an abstract class (has pure virtual function area()). Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly.

title: Order of Initialization
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Members are initialized in declaration order (a then b), not initializer list order. 'a' is initialized from uninitialized 'b'. a gets garbage value.

title: Exception in Destructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Throwing exceptions from destructors is dangerous. If another exception is already active during stack unwinding, std::terminate is called.

title: Range-based For Loop
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Range-based for loop with const auto& is valid C++11. It iterates over each element by const reference.

title: Auto Type Deduction
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: auto correctly deduces types: x is int, y is double, z is const char*. All are valid in C++11.

title: Initializer List Constructor
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Brace-enclosed initializer list for vector is valid C++11. v has 5 elements.

title: const Reference to Temporary
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: A const reference can bind to a temporary, extending its lifetime until the reference goes out of scope. This is valid.

title: Lambda Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Lambda as a comparator for std::sort is valid C++11. This sorts the vector in descending order.

title: nullptr vs NULL
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: nullptr is std::nullptr_t type (C++11). It correctly resolves to foo(int*), unlike NULL which would be ambiguous.

title: Structured Bindings
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Structured bindings (C++17) allow decomposing pairs/tuples/structs. This correctly iterates over map entries.

title: Move Semantics
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::move transfers ownership. 'b' contains 'Hello', and 'a' is in a valid but unspecified state (likely empty). Checking a.empty() is safe.

title: Enum Class
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Scoped enums (enum class) are valid C++11. They provide type safety and require the Color:: prefix.

title: Unique Pointer
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::make_unique (C++14) creates a unique_ptr. Memory is automatically freed when ptr goes out of scope.

title: Template Specialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Full template specialization for bool is valid. print(42) uses the general template, print(true) uses the bool specialization.

title: Virtual Function Override
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Virtual function with override keyword is valid C++11. Polymorphism correctly calls Derived::speak(), printing 'Derived'.

title: constexpr Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: constexpr functions can be evaluated at compile time. factorial(5) is computed at compile time, result is 120.

title: std::optional
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::optional (C++17) safely represents a value that may or may not be present. This correctly returns 4.

title: String View
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::string_view (C++17) is a non-owning view of a string. It efficiently accepts string literals without copying.

title: RAII Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::ofstream follows RAII — the file is automatically closed when it goes out of scope. No explicit close() needed.

title: static_cast Usage
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: static_cast for numeric conversion is the idiomatic C++ way. truncated is 3.

title: Deleted Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Deleted copy/assignment and private constructor implement the Singleton pattern correctly in C++11.

title: std::array
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::array is a fixed-size container wrapper. It provides bounds checking via .at() and knows its own size.

title: Multiple Catch Blocks
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Multiple catch blocks ordered from most specific to most general is valid. runtime_error is caught by the first handler.

title: If Init Statement
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: If-init statement (C++17) allows declaring variables in the if condition. The iterator 'it' is scoped to the if block.

title: Fold Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Fold expressions (C++17) allow reducing parameter packs. (args + ...) computes the right fold: 1+2+3+4+5 = 15.

title: std::variant
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::variant (C++17) is a type-safe union. The variant holds a string and std::get correctly retrieves it.

title: Inline Variable
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Inline static member variables (C++17) can be defined inside the class without a separate out-of-class definition.

title: Nodiscard Attribute
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: [[nodiscard]] (C++17) warns if the return value is discarded. Here the result is used, so no warning is generated.

title: Shared Pointer Circular Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Circular shared_ptr references (a->b->a) prevent reference counts from reaching 0. Memory is leaked. Use std::weak_ptr for one direction.

title: Map Bracket Insertion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: operator[] on a map inserts a default value (0) if the key doesn't exist. This silently creates a 'Bob' entry. Use find() or count() instead.

title: Forwarding Reference vs Rvalue Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: In a forwarding reference context, val should be forwarded with std::forward<T>(val), not used directly. Without forwarding, move semantics are not preserved.

title: String Concatenation Type Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Cannot add two string literals (const char*) with +. Use std::string("Hello") + " World" or "Hello" " World" for concatenation.

title: Missing Override Keyword
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Derived::process is missing 'const'. Without matching const, it doesn't override Base::process — it hides it. Adding 'override' would catch this at compile time.

title: Dangling Pointer from emplace_back
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: push_back may reallocate the vector's internal buffer, invalidating the reference 'ref'. Accessing it after reallocation is undefined behavior.

title: unique_ptr Copy Attempt
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: unique_ptr cannot be copied (its copy constructor is deleted). Must use std::move(ptr) to transfer ownership.

title: Thread Not Joined
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A std::thread destructor calls std::terminate if the thread is joinable. Must call t.join() or t.detach() before it goes out of scope.

title: Implicit bool Conversion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: std::cin >> name skips whitespace and blocks until non-whitespace input. It cannot produce an empty string. Use std::getline or check name.empty() after getline.

title: Const Reference in Range Loop
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: vector<bool> is a special case that returns proxy objects, not bool references. Taking a reference to a proxy is dangerous. Use 'auto b' or 'bool b' instead.

title: Exception Safety in Assignment
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Delete then new is not exception-safe: if 'new' throws, data is already deleted (dangling). Also missing self-assignment check. Use copy-and-swap idiom.

title: constexpr with Side Effects
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: constexpr functions evaluated at compile time cannot have side effects like I/O. std::cout is not allowed in a constexpr context.

title: Static Initialization Order Fiasco
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The initialization order of 'greeting' and 'message' across translation units is undefined. 'message' may use an uninitialized 'greeting'. This is the static init order fiasco.

title: SFINAE Violation
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: double_val is only enabled for integral types via enable_if. Calling with 3.14 (double) causes substitution failure → no matching function error.

title: Incorrect Mutex Lock Pattern
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Early return at line 9 exits without calling mtx.unlock(), causing a deadlock. Use std::lock_guard or std::unique_lock for RAII-based locking.

title: Catch by Value
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Catching by value slices the exception object. 'e' becomes a std::runtime_error, losing the MyError override. Catch by const reference instead.

title: Wrong auto Deduction with Braces
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: auto with brace-enclosed initializer deduces std::initializer_list<int>, not std::vector or array. This may surprise developers expecting a different type.

title: Deleted Default on POD
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: With a deleted default constructor, aggregate initialization with braces may fail in C++ standards before C++20, depending on compiler. The struct is no longer an aggregate in some versions.

title: Ambiguous Overload
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: 1L is a long. Converting long to int or double are both standard conversions of the same rank, making the call ambiguous.

title: Virtual in Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Virtual dispatch doesn't work in constructors. During Base construction, calling init() invokes Base::init(), not Derived::init(). This is a common design mistake.

title: Incorrect std::async Usage
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The returned std::future is immediately destroyed, which blocks until the async task completes (synchronous behavior). Must store the future to run truly async.

title: Returning Reference to Local Vector Element
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: v is a local vector destroyed at function end. Returning a reference to v[0] creates a dangling reference.

title: Implicit Conversion Ambiguity
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Converting A to long is ambiguous: both operator int() and operator double() could be used with an additional standard conversion to long.

title: Misuse of emplace_back
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: push_back(42) tries to construct a std::string from an int. While emplace_back(5,'a') creates "aaaaa" (valid), push_back(42) is a compile error.

title: Incorrect Use of dynamic_cast
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: dynamic_cast returns nullptr when the cast fails (a is not a Dog). Calling d->bark() on nullptr is undefined behavior. Must check if d != nullptr.

title: std::tuple
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::tuple and std::get are valid C++11. The tuple holds int, double, and const char* respectively.

title: Trailing Return Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Trailing return type syntax is valid C++11. 'auto add(int, int) -> int' is equivalent to 'int add(int, int)'.

title: std::unordered_map
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: All three insertion methods are valid for unordered_map. The size is 3.

title: constexpr if
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: if constexpr (C++17) evaluates the condition at compile time. Only the matching branch is compiled for each instantiation.

title: CTAD (Class Template Argument Deduction)
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Class Template Argument Deduction (C++17) allows constructing std::pair and std::vector without explicit template arguments.

title: Delegating Constructor
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Delegating constructors (C++11) allow one constructor to call another. Point() delegates to Point(0, 0).

title: User-Defined Literal
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: User-defined literals (C++11) allow custom suffixes. 5.5_km returns 5500.0. The underscore prefix is required for user-defined literals.

title: std::any
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::any (C++17) can hold any type. any_cast correctly retrieves the stored values. Type mismatch throws std::bad_any_cast.

title: Multiple Inheritance with override
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Multiple inheritance from interface-like abstract classes is valid and common in C++. Message correctly implements both pure virtual functions.

title: std::filesystem
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::filesystem (C++17) provides path manipulation. filename() returns 'bin', parent_path() returns '/usr/local'.

title: Aggregate Initialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: In C++14+, aggregates can have default member initializers. p.z uses its default value of 0.0.

title: noexcept Specifier
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: noexcept specifier (C++11) marks a function as non-throwing. noexcept operator returns true for noexcept functions.

title: Spaceship Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The spaceship operator <=> (C++20) provides three-way comparison. Defaulting it generates all comparison operators automatically.

title: Concepts
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Concepts (C++20) constrain template parameters. std::integral ensures T is an integer type. doubleVal(21) = 42.

title: Coroutines Generator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: This is a valid C++20 coroutine generator framework. The promise_type defines how the coroutine behaves at suspend/resume points.

title: String Literal Operator
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The 's' suffix from std::string_literals (C++14) creates a std::string directly, allowing += on the result.

title: Templated Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Templated lambdas (C++20) allow explicit template parameters in lambdas. Each call instantiates the lambda with a different type.

title: std::span
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::span (C++20) is a non-owning view of contiguous data. It can be constructed from arrays, vectors, and other contiguous containers.

title: Nested Namespace
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Nested namespace definitions (C++17) allow 'namespace A::B::C' instead of nesting three separate namespace blocks.

title: Generic Lambda with Auto
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Generic lambdas (C++14) with auto parameters work like function templates. Each call can use different types.

title: Capturing this in Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Capturing 'this' allows the lambda to access member variables. Each call increments ticks. Output: '1 2'.

title: Designated Initializers in C++20
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Designated initializers (C++20) allow naming fields in initialization. Unlike C, C++ requires the order to match the declaration order.

title: std::transform with Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::transform applies a function to each element and stores the result. Using the same range for input and output (in-place) is valid here.

title: Mutable Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Mutable lambdas can modify captured-by-value variables. The lambda's copy of 'count' increments independently. Output: '1 2 outer: 0'.

title: Placement New
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Placement new constructs an object in pre-allocated memory. Explicit destructor call is required since delete is not used. This is valid C++.

title: CRTP Wrong Template Argument
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Class B inherits from Base<A> instead of Base<B>. The CRTP static_cast will cast 'this' to A*, not B*, causing undefined behavior.

title: Template Argument Dependent Lookup
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: ADL (Argument Dependent Lookup) finds lib::process through the Widget argument. While this works, it might call an unexpected function if the user intended global::process.

title: Misuse of std::string_view Lifetime
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: string_view does not own the data. Returning a view of a local string creates a dangling view — the string is destroyed when getName returns.

title: Virtual Base Class Size
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While this compiles and runs correctly (virtual inheritance resolves the diamond), assuming sizeof(C) equals sizeof(Base) + sizeof(A) + sizeof(B) is wrong due to vptrs.

title: Incorrect Structured Binding on Map
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Map keys are const. Even though structured binding uses auto&, 'key' is actually 'const std::string&'. Modifying it is a compile error.

title: Race Condition in shared_ptr
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: shared_ptr's reference counting is thread-safe, but the pointed-to value is not. (*global_ptr)++ is a data race. Use std::atomic or a mutex.

title: Incorrect Strong Type
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Enum classes don't support arithmetic operators by default. Adding Meters and Feet is a compile error — and semantically wrong (unit mismatch).

title: Const Rvalue Reference
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Move constructor takes 'const Container&&'. std::move on a const object returns const rvalue ref, but string's move constructor needs non-const rvalue, so it copies instead of moves.

title: Wrong Template Constraint
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: static_assert fires at compile time for all instantiations. printNum(3.14) instantiates T=double, triggering the static_assert failure.

title: Incomplete Type in Container
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Using std::vector<Tree> inside Tree works in practice (since C++17 requires it), but was technically undefined in C++11/14 because Tree is incomplete at that point.

title: Infinite Template Recursion
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing template specialization for the base case (N==0 or N==1). Without it, the template recurses infinitely at compile time.

title: Condition Variable Spurious Wakeup
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: cv.wait(lock) without a predicate is vulnerable to spurious wakeups. Should use cv.wait(lock, [&]{ return ready; }) to re-check the condition.

title: Exception in noexcept Function
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Throwing from a noexcept function immediately calls std::terminate(). The catch block is never reached.

title: Incomplete Visitor Pattern
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The visitor lambda doesn't handle all variant alternatives. When op holds Sub, the function has no return statement — undefined behavior.

title: Static Variable in Header
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Static variable in a header gives each translation unit its own copy. Changes in one file won't be visible in another. Use inline (C++17) or extern.

title: Incorrect Perfect Forwarding
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Named rvalue references are lvalues. 'arg' is an lvalue even if bound to an rvalue. Use std::forward<T>(arg) to preserve value category.

title: Wrong Make Shared with Array
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: make_shared<int>(10) creates a single int initialized to 10, not an array of 10 ints. For arrays, use make_shared<int[]>(10) (C++20).

title: ODR Violation in Inline Namespace
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Two different definitions of Config in different inline namespaces violates ODR (One Definition Rule). The linker may silently use the wrong definition.

title: Destroying Move Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Deleting the moved-from object in the move constructor is catastrophic — the source object's destructor will still run, causing double-free or use-after-free.

title: Wrong comparator for std::set
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The comparator uses <= instead of <. Strict weak ordering requires that cmp(a,a) returns false. With <=, cmp(a,a) returns true, violating the requirement.

title: Lifetime Extension Failure
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: A temporary std::string is created from "Hello" and bound to the const ref return. But lifetime extension doesn't apply through function returns — dangling reference.

title: Deleted Implicitly Declared
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: std::mutex is non-copyable. Having a mutex member implicitly deletes the copy constructor for Logger. 'Logger b = a' is a compile error.

title: Template Method Not Instantiated
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: get() accesses 'data.value', which doesn't exist for int. This compiles because get() is never called — template methods are only instantiated when used. But calling c.get() would fail.

title: Dangling std::reference_wrapper
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The reference_wrappers refer to local variables a, b, c which are destroyed when getRefs returns. Accessing them later is undefined behavior.

title: Incorrect constinit Usage
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: constinit (C++20) requires constant initialization. std::string's constructor is not constexpr, so this is a compile error.

title: Ranges Pipeline
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Ranges pipelines (C++20) compose views lazily. This filters even numbers and squares them. Output: '4 16 36 64'.

title: std::expected
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::expected (C++23) holds either a value or an error. This correctly returns int on success or string on failure.

title: Aggregate with Designated Init
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Designated initializers (C++20) allow specifying individual members. Unspecified members use their default initializers (0.0f). Output: '0 1 0'.

title: static_assert with Message
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: static_assert with a message (C++11) provides compile-time type checking. Both int and double satisfy is_arithmetic.

title: Variadic Template Sum
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Variadic templates with recursive unpacking is valid C++11. The base case handles one argument, the recursive case peels off the first.

title: std::invoke
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::invoke (C++17) uniformly calls any callable, including member function pointers with objects. This correctly calls p.print(42).

title: Type Alias Template
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Template type aliases (C++11) create parameterized type aliases. Vec<int> is std::vector<int>.

title: is_same_v Type Trait
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: is_same_v (C++17) checks type equality at compile time. int == int32_t (on most platforms), int != long, and 3.14 is double.

title: Explicit Object Parameter (Deducing this)
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Deducing this (C++23) makes the implicit object parameter explicit. 'this const Widget& self' replaces the implicit const this pointer.

title: using Declaration for Base Method
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Without 'using Base::foo', Derived::foo(double) would hide Base::foo(int). The using declaration brings Base::foo into scope for overload resolution.

title: std::exchange
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::exchange (C++14) assigns a new value and returns the old one. old=10, x=42.

title: Inherited Constructors
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Inheriting constructors (C++11) with 'using Base::Base' makes Base constructors available in Derived. Derived(42) calls Base(42).

title: Consteval Function
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: consteval (C++20) forces compile-time evaluation. Unlike constexpr, square MUST be called with compile-time arguments.

title: NRVO (Named Return Value Optimization)
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: NRVO (Named Return Value Optimization) eliminates the copy/move of 'result'. The object is constructed directly in the caller's space.

title: std::scoped_lock
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::scoped_lock (C++17) locks multiple mutexes simultaneously without risk of deadlock. It uses a deadlock-avoidance algorithm.

title: std::format
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::format (C++20) provides Python-like string formatting with {} placeholders. Type-safe and extensible.

title: if consteval
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: if consteval (C++23) detects whether evaluation is happening at compile time. a=25 (compile-time), b=10 (runtime).

title: Defaulted Comparison
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Defaulted comparison operators (C++20) perform memberwise comparison. a==b is true (1), a==c is false (0).

title: std::to_array
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::to_array (C++20) creates a std::array from a C-style array or initializer list, deducing the size automatically.

title: Modules
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C++20 Modules replace headers with compiled module interfaces. 'export module' defines a module, 'import' brings it in. Faster compilation.

title: std::jthread
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::jthread (C++20) automatically joins on destruction and provides cooperative cancellation via stop_token.

title: std::source_location
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::source_location (C++20) replaces __FILE__ and __LINE__ macros with proper type-safe compile-time location info.

title: Init Capture in Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Init capture (C++14) allows moving unique_ptr into a lambda. 'p = std::move(ptr)' creates a new variable p inside the lambda.

title: Constexpr std::vector
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C++20 allows constexpr std::vector. Transient allocations (created and destroyed within constexpr context) are permitted.

title: std::bind_front
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::bind_front (C++20) binds the first arguments of a callable. timesThree(7) calls multiply(3, 7) = 21.

title: Incorrect Return of initializer_list
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The backing array of the initializer_list is a temporary. Returning it from the function creates dangling pointers — the array is destroyed at the end of the return statement.

title: Private Destructor with make_shared
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: make_shared needs to call the destructor when the reference count reaches 0. A private destructor prevents this, causing a compile error.

title: Template Template Parameter Error
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: std::vector has two template parameters (T and Allocator). The template template parameter expects one. Use 'template<typename...> class Container' or add the allocator.

title: Incorrect Comparison with NaN
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: NaN is not equal to itself (x == x is false for NaN). This is correct IEEE 754 behavior but often surprising. Use std::isnan() instead.

title: Multiple Deleters for Same Resource
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Both p1 and p2 own the same raw pointer. When p2's scope ends, it deletes the resource. p1 will try to delete it again — double free.

title: Implicit Member Access in Template
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: In a dependent base class, 'value' is not found by unqualified lookup. Use 'this->value' or 'Base<T>::value' to access it.

title: Throwing in Move Constructor
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The move constructor can throw but isn't marked noexcept. std::vector uses copy instead of move during reallocation when move can throw, losing performance benefits.

title: Accessing Variant After Bad Get
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: While this code handles the exception correctly, using std::get when the type might be wrong is inefficient. Use std::get_if or std::visit for safer access.

title: Order of Evaluation in Overloaded Operator
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The evaluation order of arguments in chained calls was unspecified before C++17. The output might not be '1 2 3'. C++17 guarantees left-to-right for member function calls.

title: Incorrect shared_from_this
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: shared_from_this() requires the object to be managed by a shared_ptr. Calling it on a stack object throws std::bad_weak_ptr or causes undefined behavior.

title: Misused std::move on const Object
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: std::move on a const object produces a const rvalue reference, which binds to the copy constructor, not the move constructor. The data is copied, not moved.

title: Conversion Operator Ambiguity
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: a == true is ambiguous: should 'a' be converted to bool (true == true) or 'true' converted to Wrapper(1) via implicit constructor? Use explicit operator bool().

title: Memory Leak in Exception Path
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: If 'new int(2)' throws, 'a' is leaked because delete a is never reached. Use smart pointers or allocate both before any can throw.

title: Erasing During Reverse Iteration
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Erasing during reverse iteration invalidates reverse iterators. The .base() conversion and subsequent erase leave iterators in an invalid state.

title: Wrong Template Default
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Template parameters with defaults must be at the end. Having T (with default) before U (without) violates the rule that defaults must trail non-defaults.

title: Accessing Uninitialized Optional
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Dereferencing an empty optional is undefined behavior. Check with has_value() or the bool conversion first, or use value() for checked access.

title: Incorrect Move Assignment Self-Check
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Missing self-assignment check: if this == &other, delete[] data destroys the data, then data = other.data assigns a dangling pointer. Add 'if (this == &other) return *this;'.

title: Undefined Behavior in Ternary Types
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The ternary branches have different types: const char* and std::string. The compiler converts const char* to string, but the auto type deduction may be surprising.

title: Misuse of reinterpret_cast
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: reinterpret_cast between unrelated types and accessing the member is undefined behavior (strict aliasing violation). Only valid for char*, byte*, or same type.

title: Wrong Priority Queue Comparator
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: This actually works correctly — std::greater creates a min-heap (top = smallest). The 'error' is often that developers expect a max-heap but get min-heap with greater<>.

title: Narrowing in Aggregate Init
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Narrowing conversion from int to unsigned char in brace initialization is ill-formed in C++11+. While 255 fits, the variable 'red' isn't a constexpr.

title: Catch Order Problem
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: The catch for std::exception comes before std::runtime_error. Since runtime_error IS an exception, the more general handler catches it first. The second catch is unreachable.

title: Capture Default with this
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: [=] captures 'this' by value (the pointer, not the object). After Widget w is destroyed, fn accesses a deleted object — undefined behavior.

title: Incorrect Concepts Constraint
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Pointers support +, but adding two pointers is meaningless (you can add pointer + int, but not pointer + pointer). The concept is too permissive.

title: Multiple Destruction via Type Erasure
description: Find the line with the error.
explanation: Capturing Heavy by value in a lambda stored in std::function causes multiple copies. Each copy/assignment of the function object copies Heavy, leading to unexpected destructor calls.

title: Three-way Comparison Result
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The spaceship operator returns a comparison category object (strong_ordering for ints). Comparing it with 0 determines the ordering. Output: 'greater'.

title: Immediately Invoked Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Immediately invoked lambda expressions (IIFE) are valid. The lambda is created and called in one expression. val = 55.

title: Extended friend Declarations
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Friend class declarations grant the friend access to private members. Printer can access Data::value. This is a valid pattern.

title: std::apply with Tuple
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::apply (C++17) unpacks a tuple as arguments to a callable. This calls add(1, 2, 3) = 6.

title: Strong Type with Enum
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Scoped enum with explicit underlying type is valid. Wednesday = 3 (auto-incremented from Monday = 1).

title: constexpr Lambda
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Constexpr lambdas (C++17) can be evaluated at compile time. static_assert verifies square(5) at compile time.

title: Override with Different Access
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: A virtual function can be overridden with a different access specifier. Access is checked at compile time based on the static type — Base* allows calling method().

title: empty() on Container
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: empty() checks if the container has no elements. This is valid and preferred over size() == 0. Output: 'Empty Not empty'.

title: std::clamp
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::clamp (C++17) constrains a value to a range. 150 is clamped to [0, 100], resulting in 100.

title: Copy Elision Guaranteed
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C++17 guarantees copy elision for prvalue initialization. Even with deleted copy/move constructors, 'Widget w = create()' works because no copy/move is needed.

title: std::make_from_tuple
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::make_from_tuple (C++17) constructs an object from a tuple of arguments. This creates Point(10, 20).

title: Lambda as Default Argument
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Lambdas can be used as default arguments through std::function. greet() uses the default, greet(lambda) uses the custom one.

title: Partial Template Specialization
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Partial template specialization matches when both types are the same. p1 uses the general template, p2 uses the specialized one.

title: Static Local Thread Safe
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C++11 guarantees thread-safe initialization of local static variables (Meyers' Singleton). The static local is initialized exactly once.

title: Erase-Remove Idiom
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The erase-remove idiom efficiently removes all occurrences of 2. remove shifts elements, erase removes the tail. Output: '1 3 4 5'.

title: Using enum in Scope
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: using enum (C++20) brings all enum values into scope. After 'using enum Color', you can write 'Red' instead of 'Color::Red'.

title: Spaceship with Custom Logic
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Defaulted <=> compares members in declaration order. (1,2,3) < (1,3,0) because when major is equal, minor 2 < 3. Output: 'older'.

title: Structured Binding with Custom Type
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Structured bindings work with any aggregate or class with public members. The fields are bound in declaration order.

title: std::reduce
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: std::reduce (C++17) is similar to accumulate but allows parallel execution. For addition with 0 init, it computes the sum = 15.

title: Constexpr String
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: C++20 allows constexpr std::string for transient use. The allocation happens and is freed within the constexpr evaluation.

title: Explicit Bool Conversion
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Explicit bool conversion allows use in boolean contexts (if, while, &&, ||, !) but prevents implicit conversion to int or other types.

title: Inline Namespace for Versioning
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Inline namespaces make their members accessible from the enclosing namespace. lib::greet() calls v2, lib::v1::greet() calls v1.

title: Deduction Guide
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: User-defined deduction guides (C++17) control CTAD. The guide makes Wrapper("hello") create Wrapper<std::string> instead of Wrapper<const char*>.

title: std::visit Overload Pattern
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: The overloaded pattern combines multiple lambdas for std::visit. CTAD deduces the template arguments. This is a common C++17 variant visitation pattern.

title: Requires Expression
description: Is this code snippet correct?
explanation: Requires expressions (C++20) define constraints. The Printable concept checks that os << t returns std::ostream&. Both int and const char* satisfy this.
